meta.toolResultPreview: 1: # colorful-times v2 — Design Spec 2: 3: **Date:** 2026-03-22 4: **Status:** Approved 5: **Neovim minimum:** 0.12 6: **External dependency:** snacks.nvim (optional — TUI degrades gracefully without it) 7: 8: --- 9: 10: ## 1. Goals 11: 12: Full rewrite of colorful-times.nvim targeting Neovim 0.12+. The rewrite: 13: 14: - Drops all compatibility shims for Neovim < 0.12 15: - Restructures into focused, independently-testable modules 16: - Adds a keyboard-driven TUI (Table Manager) for managing schedules at runtime 17: - Persists TUI-made schedule changes to disk so they survive restarts 18: - Pauses the appearance poll timer when Neovim loses focus (zero CPU overhead when idle) 19: - Uses sna…

Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking. 4: 5: **Goal:** Full rewrite of colorful-times.nvim for Neovim 0.12+, splitting the monolithic `impl.lua` into focused modules, adding a snacks-powered TUI for schedule management, and persisting TUI changes to disk. 6: 7: **Architecture:** Seven focused modules (`init`, `schedule`, `system`, `state`, `core`, `tui`, `health`) plus a thin `plugin/` entry point. meta.toolResultPreview: 1: *colorful-times.txt* For Neovim version 0.12+ Last change: 2026 2: 3: ============================================================================== 4: COLORFUL-TIMES *colorful-times* 5: 6: Colorful Times is a fast and simple Neovim plugin that automatically sets 7: your colorscheme based on your system appearance (light/dark mode) or based 8: on a time-of-day schedule. It is highly optimized, ensuring zero impact on 9: Neovim startup times via asynchronous `vim.uv` module spawning. 10: 11: ============================================================================== 12: CONTENTS *colorful-times-contents* 13: 14: 1. Introduction .......................... |colorful-times-introduction| 15…

vision.rationale: The v2 implementation plan document clearly articulates the project vision: a full rewrite for Neovim 0.12+ with modular architecture, TUI schedule management, and persistent state. The design spec elaborates on goals including dropping compatibility shims, adding snacks-powered TUI, and focus-aware timer pausing. The help doc provides user-facing vision of seamless dark/light mode matching with zero startup overhead.

architecture.rationale: The v2 design spec provides extremely detailed architecture documentation including module structure, module contracts with function signatures and type definitions, timer logic, state persistence schema, TUI design with keymaps, and the two-phase colorscheme application strategy. AGENTS.md supplements with file structure overview.

principles_guidelines.rationale: AGENTS.md contains comprehensive code style rules covering formatting, imports, naming conventions, annotations, error handling, async patterns, Vim API preferences, iteration patterns, and performance guidelines. The design spec adds architectural principles like module purity for schedule.lua and lazy-loading requirements for init.lua.
